IF FORT I DOES NOT PAY $49,000 TO PLAINTIFFS ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 18, 2017, PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE THEIR PROPOSED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 29, 2017. Notice Of Motion For An Order Compelling Defendants' Depositions And Written Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-30-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS JUVENAL VIRRUETE, JOAQUIN DELGADO, DANIEL GONZALES' Motion For An Order Compelling Defendants' Depositions And Written Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

The motion of plaintiffs for an order compelling defendants' depositions and written discovery responses is unopposed and shall be granted in its entirety, with full compliance due without objection within thirty days of the order hereon. Defendants and their counsel shall further pay, jointly and severally, sanctions for discovery abuse to plaintiffs in the amount of $2510.00, within ten days of the order hereon.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Re-Notice Of Motion; Motion For Order Granting Plaintiff Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-08-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alexei Kuchinsky, Esq. of Klein Law Group, Tel. 415.693.9107, for plaintiffs and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS JUVENAL VIRRUETE, JOAQUIN DELGADO and DANIEL GONZALES' Re-Noticed Motion For Order Granting Plaintiffs Leave To File Second Amended Complaint as follows: Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is denied on the condition that on or before September 18, 2017 defendant Fort I, LLC pay $49,000 (the amount of the accepted CCP 998 offer) to plaintiffs. If Fort I does not pay $49,000 to plaintiffs on or before September 18, 2017, plaintiffs are granted leave to file their proposed second amended complaint on or before September 29, 2017. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint By Defendant Fort 1, Llc
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FORT 1, LLC'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint By Defendant Fort 1, LLC, as to the punitive damages allegations is DENIED. The punitive damages allegations are sufficiently alleged for this early stage of the case. Defendant's remedy, if any, is to take propound appropriate discovery and, if appropriate, file a motion for summary adjudication. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
